title: Transport Layer Security
description: Cryptographic protocol providing encrypted, authenticated communication over the internet.
fullExplanation: TLS runs on top of TCP and provides three security properties: Confidentiality (data encrypted with AES-256-GCM), Integrity (HMAC or AEAD tag detects tampering), Authentication (X.509 certificates prove server identity). TLS 1.3 simplified the handshake to 1 RTT (vs 2 RTT in TLS 1.2), removed weak algorithms, and mandates forward secrecy via ECDHE key exchange.

handshake13.name: ClientHello
handshake13.description: Supported cipher suites, key share (X25519), TLS version

handshake13.name: ServerHello
handshake13.description: Chosen cipher, key share, certificate, Finished MAC

handshake13.name: Client Finished
handshake13.description: Verify server cert, send Finished MAC. Keys derived.

keyFacts: ChaCha20-Poly1305: faster on mobile without AES hardware
TLS 1.3 removed: RSA key exchange, CBC mode, MD5/SHA-1
0-RTT resumption: replay attack risk — use carefully
Certificate Transparency: all certs must be publicly logged
HSTS: forces HTTPS for up to 2 years
